episode_summary: This episode covers Sami shamans secretly practicing with drums in 17th-century Sweden, the Loebner Prize’s “most human human” award in Turing test contests, France’s reenactment-based theme park Puy du Fou, and John Williams’s stated dislike of film music. Along the way they discuss drum languages, AI and CAPTCHAs, driverless cars, and historical reenactments.

facts.fact: Okay, my fact this week is that in 17th century Sweden, Sami Shaman's had to work in secret. Unfortunately, most of their work involved constant drumming.
My fact is, from 1990 to 2018, there was an award given out every year for the most human human.
My fact this week is that the only ride at France's second most popular theme park is a two-year-old caracel.
My fact this week is that John Williams, the composer of soundtracks, like Jaws, Star Wars and Indiana Jones, doesn't actually like film music.
